title: UniVideo: Unified Understanding, Generation, and Editing for Videos
core_contribution: UniVideo proposes a dual-stream architecture (frozen MLLM + MMDiT) that unifies video understanding, generation, and editing under a single multimodal instruction paradigm, using a 3-stage training strategy and demonstrating zero-shot generalization to unseen tasks and task compositions.
summary: UniVideo combines a frozen MLLM (Qwen2.5VL-7B) for instruction understanding with an MMDiT (HunyuanVideo-13B) for video generation, connected through MLP connector using self-attention alignment. A 3-stage training strategy enables T2V, I2V, in-context video generation, and editing from a single model. It achieves 0.88 subject consistency in in-context generation (vs. 0.68 for Kling1.6) and demonstrates zero-shot generalization from image editing to free-form video editing.
key_findings: MMDiT self-attention alignment is superior to cross-attention for connecting MLLM with diffusion generator
Multi-task training significantly improves over single-task baselines
Dual-branch visual input is critical — removing visual input to MMDiT causes SC to drop from 0.78 to 0.18
Zero-shot generalization from image editing to video editing emerges from unified training
3D positional embeddings outperform MRoPE for distinguishing condition vs. noisy latents
UniVideo is the only model supporting understanding + generation + editing + in-context generation simultaneously
limitations: Struggles to fully preserve motion of original videos due to backbone limitations
Free-form video editing success rate lower than image editing
Post-trained assembled system rather than native end-to-end multimodal model

infra_impact.algorithm: New paradigm for unified multi-task video model training; self-attention > cross-attention for MLLM-DiT alignment
infra_impact.framework: Serving dual-stream MLLM+MMDiT requires handling variable multimodal inputs and dynamic sequence lengths
infra_impact.llm: Demonstrates effective strategy for leveraging frozen MLLMs as multimodal encoders for generation
infra_impact.agent: Visual prompting capability enables agents to interpret hand-drawn annotations for video generation

open_questions: How to maintain understanding quality when training MLLM end-to-end with generation?
Can the self-attention alignment pattern scale to 70B+ MLLM models?
How to achieve precise spatial grounding without mask inputs for video editing?
